NRSV.label: New Revised Standard Version
NRSV.rights: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

KJV.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

NABRE.note: Declared during NT systemic contamination cleanup.

text.NRSV: Paul and Timothy, servants of Christ Jesus, To all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with the bishops and deacons:
text.Rotherham: Paul and Timothy, servants of Jesus Christ,—unto all the saints in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with overseers and ministers:—
text.KJV: Paul and Timotheus, the servants of Jesus Christ, to all the saints in Christ Jesus which are at Philippi, with the bishops and deacons:
text.NABRE: I. Address Chapter 1 Greeting. Paul and Timothy, slaves of Christ Jesus, to all the holy ones in Christ Jesus who are in Philippi, with the overseers and ministers:
text.DRB: Paul and Timothee the servants of Jesus Christ; to all the saints in Christ Jesus that are at Philippi, with the Bishops and Deacons.

text.NRSV: Grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Favour unto you, and peace, from God our Father and Lord Jesus Christ.
text.KJV: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: grace to you and peace from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. Thanksgiving.
text.DRB: Grace to you and peace from God our Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: I thank my God every time I remember you,
text.Rotherham: I am giving thanks unto my God, on occasion of all my remembrance of you,
text.KJV: I thank my God upon every remembrance of you,
text.NABRE: I give thanks to my God at every remembrance of you,
text.DRB: I give thanks to my God in all memory of you

text.NRSV: constantly in every prayer of mine for all of you, praying with joy
text.Rotherham: At all times, in every supplication of mine, in behalf of you all, with joy, my supplication, making,—
text.KJV: Always in every prayer of mine for you all making request with joy,
text.NABRE: praying always with joy in my every prayer for all of you,
text.DRB: (always in all my prayers for all you, with joy making petition)

text.NRSV: because of your sharing in the gospel from the first day until now.
text.Rotherham: On account of your contribution unto the glad-message, from the first day until the present:
text.KJV: For your fellowship in the gospel from the first day until now;
text.NABRE: because of your partnership for the gospel from the first day until now.
text.DRB: for your communicating in the Gospel of Christ from the first day until now.

text.NRSV: I am confident of this, that the one who began a good work among you will bring it to completion by the day of Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: Being persuaded of this very thing—that, he who hath begun in you a good work, will perfect it, until the day of Jesus Christ;
text.KJV: Being confident of this very thing, that he which hath begun a good work in you will perform it until the day of Jesus Christ:
text.NABRE: I am confident of this, that the one who began a good work in you will continue to complete it until the day of Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: trusting this same thing, that he which hath begun in you a good work, will perfit it unto the day of Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: It is right for me to think this way about all of you, because you hold me in your heart, for all of you share in God’s grace with me, both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel.
text.Rotherham: According as it is right in me to have this regard in behalf of you all,—because ye have had me in your hearts, both in my bonds and in the defence and confirmation of the glad-message, all of you being, joint partakers of my favour.
text.KJV: Even as it is meet for me to think this of you all, because I have you in my heart; inasmuch as both in my bonds, and in the defence and confirmation of the gospel, ye all are partakers of my grace.
text.NABRE: It is right that I should think this way about all of you, because I hold you in my heart, you who are all partners with me in grace, both in my imprisonment and in the defense and confirmation of the gospel.
text.DRB: As it is reason for me, this to think for all you, for that I have you in heart, & in my bands, and in the defence, and the confirmation of the Gospel, all you to be partakers of my joy.

text.NRSV: For God is my witness, how I long for all of you with the compassion of Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: For God is, my witness, how I long for you all in the tender affections of Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: For God is my record, how greatly I long after you all in the bowels of Jesus Christ.
text.NABRE: For God is my witness, how I long for all of you with the affection of Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: For God is my witness, how I covet you all in the bowels of Jesus Christ.

text.NRSV: And this is my prayer, that your love may overflow more and more with knowledge and full insight
text.Rotherham: And, this, I pray—that, your love, may be, yet more and more, pre-eminent in personal knowledge and all perception,
text.KJV: And this I pray, that your love may abound yet more and more in knowledge and in all judgment;
text.NABRE: And this is my prayer: that your love may increase ever more and more in knowledge and every kind of perception,
text.DRB: And this I pray, that your charity may more and more abound in knowledge and in all understanding:

text.NRSV: to help you determine what is best, so that in the day of Christ you may be pure and blameless,
text.Rotherham: To the end ye may be putting to the test the things that differ, in order that ye may be incorrupt and may give no occasion of stumbling, unto the day of Christ,
text.KJV: That ye may approve things that are excellent; that ye may be sincere and without offence till the day of Christ;
text.NABRE: to discern what is of value, so that you may be pure and blameless for the day of Christ,
text.DRB: that you may approve the better things, that you may be sincere and without offence unto the day of Christ,

text.NRSV: having produced the harvest of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ for the glory and praise of God.
text.Rotherham: Filled with that fruit of righteousness which is through Jesus Christ unto the glory and praise of God.
text.KJV: Being filled with the fruits of righteousness, which are by Jesus Christ, unto the glory and praise of God.
text.NABRE: filled with the fruit of righteousness that comes through Jesus Christ for the glory and praise of God.
text.DRB: replenished with the fruit of justice by Jesus Christ, unto the glory and praise of God.

text.NRSV: I want you to know, beloved, that what has happened to me has actually helped to spread the gospel,
text.Rotherham: Howbeit, I am minded, brethren, that ye should be, getting to know, that, the things which relate unto myself, rather, unto an advancement of the glad-message, have fallen out,
text.KJV: But I would ye should understand, brethren, that the things which happened unto me have fallen out rather unto the furtherance of the gospel;
text.NABRE: II. Progress of the Gospel I want you to know, brothers, that my situation has turned out rather to advance the gospel,
text.DRB: And I will have you know, brethren, that the things about me are come to the more furtherance of the Gospel:

text.NRSV: so that it has become known throughout the whole imperial guard and to everyone else that my imprisonment is for Christ;
text.Rotherham: So that, my bonds, have become manifest in Christ, in the whole palace, and unto all the rest,—
text.KJV: So that my bonds in Christ are manifest in all the palace, and in all other places;
text.NABRE: so that my imprisonment has become well known in Christ throughout the whole praetorium and to all the rest,
text.DRB: so that my bands were made manifest in Christ in all the court, and in all the rest,

text.NRSV: and most of the brothers and sisters, having been made confident in the Lord by my imprisonment, dare to speak the word with greater boldness and without fear.
text.Rotherham: And [so that], the most of the brethren in the Lord, assured by my bonds, are becoming more abundantly bold to be fearlessly speaking the word of God:—
text.KJV: And many of the brethren in the Lord, waxing confident by my bonds, are much more bold to speak the word without fear.
text.NABRE: and so that the majority of the brothers, having taken encouragement in the Lord from my imprisonment, dare more than ever to proclaim the word fearlessly.
text.DRB: that many of our brethren in our Lord, having confidence in my bands, were bold more aboundantly without fear to speak the word of God.

text.NRSV: Some proclaim Christ from envy and rivalry, but others from goodwill.
text.Rotherham: Some, indeed, by reason of envy and strife—some, however, by reason of good will—are proclaiming, the Christ:
text.KJV: Some indeed preach Christ even of envy and strife; and some also of good will:
text.NABRE: Of course, some preach Christ from envy and rivalry, others from good will.
text.DRB: Some indeed even for envy and contention: but some also for good will preach Christ.

text.NRSV: These proclaim Christ out of love, knowing that I have been put here for the defense of the gospel;
text.Rotherham: These, indeed, out of love, knowing that, for the defence of the glad-message, I am set;
text.KJV: The one preach Christ of contention, not sincerely, supposing to add affliction to my bonds:
text.NABRE: The latter act out of love, aware that I am here for the defense of the gospel;
text.DRB: Some of charity: knowing that I am set unto the defence of the Gospel.

text.NRSV: the others proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition, not sincerely but intending to increase my suffering in my imprisonment.
text.Rotherham: But, those, out of faction, are declaring, the Christ, not purely—supposing to rouse up, tribulation, with my bonds.
text.KJV: But the other of love, knowing that I am set for the defence of the gospel.
text.NABRE: the former proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition, not from pure motives, thinking that they will cause me trouble in my imprisonment.
text.DRB: And some of contention preach Christ not sincerely: supposing that they raise affliction to my bands.

text.NRSV: What does it matter? Just this, that Christ is proclaimed in every way, whether out of false motives or true; and in that I rejoice. Yes, and I will continue to rejoice,
text.Rotherham: What then? That, in any way, whether in pretext, or in truth, Christ, is declared, and, in this, I rejoice—yea, and will rejoice!
text.KJV: What then? notwithstanding, every way, whether in pretence, or in truth, Christ is preached; and I therein do rejoice, yea, and will rejoice.
text.NABRE: What difference does it make, as long as in every way, whether in pretense or in truth, Christ is being proclaimed? And in that I rejoice. Indeed I shall continue to rejoice,
text.DRB: But what? So that by all means, whether by occasion, or by truth, Christ be preached: in this also I rejoice, yea & will rejoice.

text.NRSV: for I know that through your prayers and the help of the Spirit of Jesus Christ this will turn out for my deliverance.
text.Rotherham: For I know that, this, shall turn unto me for salvation—through your supplication, and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: For I know that this shall turn to my salvation through your prayer, and the supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,
text.NABRE: for I know that this will result in deliverance for me through your prayers and support from the Spirit of Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: For I know that this shall fall out to me unto salvation by your prayer and the subministration of the Spirit of Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: It is my eager expectation and hope that I will not be put to shame in any way, but that by my speaking with all boldness, Christ will be exalted now as always in my body, whether by life or by death.
text.Rotherham: According to my eager outlook and hope, that, in nothing, shall I be put to shame, but, with all freedom of speech, as always, now also, shall Christ be magnified in my body, whether through means of life, or of death.
text.KJV: According to my earnest expectation and my hope, that in nothing I shall be ashamed, but that with all boldness, as always, so now also Christ shall be magnified in my body, whether it be by life, or by death.
text.NABRE: My eager expectation and hope is that I shall not be put to shame in any way, but that with all boldness, now as always, Christ will be magnified in my body, whether by life or by death.
text.DRB: according to my expectation & hope; because in nothing shall I be confounded, but in all confidence as always, now also shall Christ be magnified in my body, whether it be by life, or by death.

text.NRSV: For to me, living is Christ and dying is gain.
text.Rotherham: For, unto me—living, is Christ, and, dying, gain.
text.KJV: For to me to live is Christ, and to die is gain.
text.NABRE: For to me life is Christ, and death is gain.
text.DRB: For unto me, to live is Christ: and to die is gain.

text.NRSV: If I am to live in the flesh, that means fruitful labor for me; and I do not know which I prefer.
text.Rotherham: But, if living in flesh, [is Christ], this, unto me, is a fruit of work,—and, what I shall choose, I make not known:
text.KJV: But if I live in the flesh, this is the fruit of my labour: yet what I shall choose I wot not.
text.NABRE: If I go on living in the flesh, that means fruitful labor for me. And I do not know which I shall choose.
text.DRB: And if to live in the flesh, this unto me be the fruit of the work, and what I shall choose I know not.

text.NRSV: I am hard pressed between the two: my desire is to depart and be with Christ, for that is far better;
text.Rotherham: I am held in constraint, however, by reason of the two,—having, the coveting, to be released, and to be with, Christ, for it were far better!
text.KJV: For I am in a strait betwixt two, having a desire to depart, and to be with Christ; which is far better:
text.NABRE: I am caught between the two. I long to depart this life and be with Christ, [for] that is far better.
text.DRB: And I am straitened of the two: having desire to be dissolved & to be with Christ, a thing much more better.

text.NRSV: but to remain in the flesh is more necessary for you.
text.Rotherham: But, to abide still in the flesh, is more needful for your sake;
text.KJV: Nevertheless to abide in the flesh is more needful for you.
text.NABRE: Yet that I remain [in] the flesh is more necessary for your benefit.
text.DRB: But to abide in the flesh, necessary for you.

text.NRSV: Since I am convinced of this, I know that I will remain and continue with all of you for your progress and joy in faith,
text.Rotherham: And, of, this, being assured, know that I shall abide,—yea abide with you all, for your advancement, and the joy of your faith;
text.KJV: And having this confidence, I know that I shall abide and continue with you all for your furtherance and joy of faith;
text.NABRE: And this I know with confidence, that I shall remain and continue in the service of all of you for your progress and joy in the faith,
text.DRB: And trusting this, I know that I shall abide and continue with you all, unto your furtherance and joy of the faith:

text.NRSV: so that I may share abundantly in your boasting in Christ Jesus when I come to you again.
text.Rotherham: In order that, your matter of boasting, may be more abundant in Christ Jesus, regarding me, through my own presence again with you.
text.KJV: That your rejoicing may be more abundant in Jesus Christ for me by my coming to you again.
text.NABRE: so that your boasting in Christ Jesus may abound on account of me when I come to you again. III. Instructions for the Community Steadfastness in Faith.
text.DRB: that your gratulation may abound in Christ Jesus in me, by my coming again to you.

text.NRSV: Only, live your life in a manner worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that, whether I come and see you or am absent and hear about you, I will know that you are standing firm in one spirit, striving side by side with one mind for the faith of the gospel,
text.Rotherham: Only, in a manner worthy of the glad-message of the Christ, be using your citizenship; in order that—whether coming and seeing you, or being absent, I may hear of the things which concern you—that ye are standing fast in one spirit, with one soul, joining for the combat along with the faith of the glad-message;
text.KJV: Only let your conversation be as it becometh the gospel of Christ: that whether I come and see you, or else be absent, I may hear of your affairs, that ye stand fast in one spirit, with one mind striving together for the faith of the gospel;
text.NABRE: Only, conduct yourselves in a way worthy of the gospel of Christ, so that, whether I come and see you or am absent, I may hear news of you, that you are standing firm in one spirit, with one mind struggling together for the faith of the gospel,
text.DRB: Only converse ye worthy of the Gospel of Christ: that whether when I come and see you, or else be absent, I may hear of you that you stand in one Spirit, of one mind labouring together to the faith of the Gospel.

text.NRSV: and are in no way intimidated by your opponents. For them this is evidence of their destruction, but of your salvation. And this is God’s doing.
text.Rotherham: And not being affrighted in anything by the opposers,—the which, is, unto them, a token of destruction, although of your salvation, and this from God;
text.KJV: And in nothing terrified by your adversaries: which is to them an evident token of perdition, but to you of salvation, and that of God.
text.NABRE: not intimidated in any way by your opponents. This is proof to them of destruction, but of your salvation. And this is God’s doing.
text.DRB: And in nothing be ye terrified of the adversaries, which to them is cause of perdition: but to you of salvation, & this of God:

text.NRSV: For he has graciously granted you the privilege not only of believing in Christ, but of suffering for him as well—
text.Rotherham: Because, unto you, hath it been given as a favour, in behalf of Christ, not only, on him, to believe, but also, in his behalf, to suffer:—
text.KJV: For unto you it is given in the behalf of Christ, not only to believe on him, but also to suffer for his sake;
text.NABRE: For to you has been granted, for the sake of Christ, not only to believe in him but also to suffer for him.
text.DRB: for to you it is given for Christ, not only that you believe in him, but also that you suffer for him,

text.NRSV: since you are having the same struggle that you saw I had and now hear that I still have.
text.Rotherham: Having, the same contest, which ye have seen in me, and now hear to be in me.
text.KJV: Having the same conflict which ye saw in me, and now hear to be in me.
text.NABRE: Yours is the same struggle as you saw in me and now hear about me.
text.DRB: having the same combat like as you have seen in me, and now have heard of me.

text.NRSV: If then there is any encouragement in Christ, any consolation from love, any sharing in the Spirit, any compassion and sympathy,
text.Rotherham: If there be, therefore, any encouragement in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of spirit, if any tender affections and compassions,
text.KJV: If there be therefore any consolation in Christ, if any comfort of love, if any fellowship of the Spirit, if any bowels and mercies,
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 Plea for Unity and Humility. If there is any encouragement in Christ, any solace in love, any participation in the Spirit, any compassion and mercy,
text.DRB: IF therefore there be any consolation in Christ, if any solace of charity, if any society of spirit, if any bowels of commiseration;

text.NRSV: make my joy complete: be of the same mind, having the same love, being in full accord and of one mind.
text.Rotherham: Fill ye up my joy—that, the same thing, ye esteem, the same love, possessing, joined in soul, the one thing, esteeming,—
text.KJV: Fulfil ye my joy, that ye be likeminded, having the same love, being of one accord, of one mind.
text.NABRE: complete my joy by being of the same mind, with the same love, united in heart, thinking one thing.
text.DRB: fulfill my joy, that you be of one meaning, having the same charity, of one mind, agreeing in one.

text.NRSV: Do nothing from selfish ambition or conceit, but in humility regard others as better than yourselves.
text.Rotherham: Nothing by way of faction, nothing by way of vain-glory,—but, in lowliness of mind, accounting, one another, superior to yourselves,—
text.KJV: Let nothing be done through strife or vainglory; but in lowliness of mind let each esteem other better than themselves.
text.NABRE: Do nothing out of selfishness or out of vainglory; rather, humbly regard others as more important than yourselves,
text.DRB: Nothing by contention, neither by vain glory: but in humility, each counting other better than themselves:

text.NRSV: Let each of you look not to your own interests, but to the interests of others.
text.Rotherham: Not, to your own things, severally looking, but, to the things of others, severally:—
text.KJV: Look not every man on his own things, but every man also on the things of others.
text.NABRE: each looking out not for his own interests, but [also] everyone for those of others.
text.DRB: every one not considering the things that are their own, but those that are other men's.

text.NRSV: Let the same mind be in you that was in Christ Jesus,
text.Rotherham: The same thing, esteem, in yourselves, which also, in Christ Jesus, [ye esteem],—
text.KJV: Let this mind be in you, which was also in Christ Jesus:
text.NABRE: Have among yourselves the same attitude that is also yours in Christ Jesus,
text.DRB: For this think in yourselves, which also in Christ Jesus;

text.NRSV: who, though he was in the form of God, did not regard equality with God as something to be exploited,
text.Rotherham: Who, in form of God, subsisting, not, a thing to be seized, accounted the being equal with God,
text.KJV: Who, being in the form of God, thought it not robbery to be equal with God:
text.NABRE: Who, though he was in the form of God, did not regard equality with God something to be grasped.
text.DRB: who when he was in the form of God, thought it no robbery, himself to be equal to God:

text.NRSV: but emptied himself, taking the form of a slave, being born in human likeness. And being found in human form,
text.Rotherham: But, himself, emptied, taking, a servant’s form, coming to be, in men’s likeness;
text.KJV: But made himself of no reputation, and took upon him the form of a servant, and was made in the likeness of men:
text.NABRE: Rather, he emptied himself, taking the form of a slave, coming in human likeness; and found human in appearance,
text.DRB: but he exinanited himself, taking the form of a servant, made into the similitude of men, and in shape found as a man.

text.NRSV: he humbled himself and became obedient to the point of death— even death on a cross.
text.Rotherham: And, in fashion, being found, as a man, humbled himself, becoming obedient as far as death, yea, death upon a cross.
text.KJV: And being found in fashion as a man, he humbled himself, and became obedient unto death, even the death of the cross.
text.NABRE: he humbled himself, becoming obedient to death, even death on a cross.
text.DRB: He humbled himself, made obedient unto death: even the death of the cross.

text.NRSV: Therefore God also highly exalted him and gave him the name that is above every name,
text.Rotherham: Wherefore also, God, uplifted him far on high, and favoured him with the name which is above every name,—
text.KJV: Wherefore God also hath highly exalted him, and given him a name which is above every name:
text.NABRE: Because of this, God greatly exalted him and bestowed on him the name that is above every name,
text.DRB: For the which thing God also hath exalted him, and hath given him a name which is above all names:

text.NRSV: so that at the name of Jesus every knee should bend, in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
text.Rotherham: In order that, in the name of Jesus, every knee might bow—of beings in heaven, and on earth, and underground,—
text.KJV: That at the name of Jesus every knee should bow, of things in heaven, and things in earth, and things under the earth;
text.NABRE: that at the name of Jesus every knee should bend, of those in heaven and on earth and under the earth,
text.DRB: that in the name of Jesus every knee bow of the celestials, terrestrials, and infernals:

text.NRSV: and every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
text.Rotherham: And, every tongue, might openly confess—that Jesus Christ is, Lord, unto the glory of God the Father.
text.KJV: And that every tongue should confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father.
text.NABRE: and every tongue confess that Jesus Christ is Lord, to the glory of God the Father. Obedience and Service in the World.
text.DRB: and every tongue confess that our Lord Jesus Christ is in the glory of God the Father.

text.NRSV: Therefore, my beloved, just as you have always obeyed me, not only in my presence, but much more now in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling;
text.Rotherham: So, then, my beloved—even as ye have always obeyed, not [as] in my presence only, but, now, much more, in my absence, with fear and trembling, your own salvation, be working out;
text.KJV: Wherefore, my beloved, as ye have always obeyed, not as in my presence only, but now much more in my absence, work out your own salvation with fear and trembling.
text.NABRE: So then, my beloved, obedient as you have always been, not only when I am present but all the more now when I am absent, work out your salvation with fear and trembling.
text.DRB: Therefore, my Dearest, (as you have always obeyed) not as in the presence of me only, but much more now in my absence, with fear and trembling work your salvation.

text.NRSV: for it is God who is at work in you, enabling you both to will and to work for his good pleasure.
text.Rotherham: For it is, God, who energiseth within you, both the desiring and the energising, in behalf of his good pleasure.
text.KJV: For it is God which worketh in you both to will and to do of his good pleasure.
text.NABRE: For God is the one who, for his good purpose, works in you both to desire and to work.
text.DRB: For it is God that worketh in you both to will and to accomplish, according to his good will.

text.NRSV: Do all things without murmuring and arguing,
text.Rotherham: All things, be doing, apart from murmurings and disputings;
text.KJV: Do all things without murmurings and disputings:
text.NABRE: Do everything without grumbling or questioning,
text.DRB: And do ye all things without murmurings and staggerings:

text.NRSV: so that you may be blameless and innocent, children of God without blemish in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, in which you shine like stars in the world.
text.Rotherham: In order that ye may become faultless and inviolate, children of God, blameless amidst a crooked and perverted generation, amongst whom ye appear as luminaries in the world,
text.KJV: That ye may be blameless and harmless, the sons of God, without rebuke, in the midst of a crooked and perverse nation, among whom ye shine as lights in the world;
text.NABRE: that you may be blameless and innocent, children of God without blemish in the midst of a crooked and perverse generation, among whom you shine like lights in the world,
text.DRB: that you may be without blame, and the simple children of God, without reprehension in the midst of a crooked and perverse Generation. Among whom you shine as lights in the world:

text.NRSV: It is by your holding fast to the word of life that I can boast on the day of Christ that I did not run in vain or labor in vain.
text.Rotherham: A word of light, holding forth,—as a matter of boasting unto me, for the day of Christ,—that, not in vain, I ran, nor, in vain, I toiled:—
text.KJV: Holding forth the word of life; that I may rejoice in the day of Christ, that I have not run in vain, neither laboured in vain.
text.NABRE: as you hold on to the word of life, so that my boast for the day of Christ may be that I did not run in vain or labor in vain.
text.DRB: containing the word of life to my glory in the day of Christ, because I have not run in vain, nor in vain laboured.

text.NRSV: But even if I am being poured out as a libation over the sacrifice and the offering of your faith, I am glad and rejoice with all of you—
text.Rotherham: Nay! if I am even to be poured out as a drink-offering, upon the sacrifice and public ministry of your faith, I rejoice, yea rejoice together with you all,—
text.KJV: Yea, and if I be offered upon the sacrifice and service of your faith, I joy, and rejoice with you all.
text.NABRE: But, even if I am poured out as a libation upon the sacrificial service of your faith, I rejoice and share my joy with all of you.
text.DRB: But and if I be immolated, upon the Sacrifice and service of your faith, I rejoice and congratulate with you all.

text.NRSV: and in the same way you also must be glad and rejoice with me.
text.Rotherham: For the same cause, moreover, do, ye also, rejoice, yea rejoice together with me.
text.KJV: For the same cause also do ye joy, and rejoice with me.
text.NABRE: In the same way you also should rejoice and share your joy with me.
text.DRB: And the self-same thing do you also rejoice, and congratulate with me.

text.NRSV: I hope in the Lord Jesus to send Timothy to you soon, so that I may be cheered by news of you.
text.Rotherham: I am hoping, however, in the Lord Jesus—Timothy, shortly, to send unto you, in order that, I also, may be of cheerful soul, when I have ascertained the things that concern you.
text.KJV: But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus shortly unto you, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know your state.
text.NABRE: IV. Travel Plans of Paul and His Assistants Timothy and Paul. I hope, in the Lord Jesus, to send Timothy to you soon, so that I too may be heartened by hearing news of you.
text.DRB: And I hope in our Lord Jesus, to send Timothee unto you quickly, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know the things pertaining to you.

text.NRSV: I have no one like him who will be genuinely concerned for your welfare.
text.Rotherham: For, no one, have I, of equal soul, who, genuinely, as to the things that concern you, will be anxious;
text.KJV: For I have no man likeminded, who will naturally care for your state.
text.NABRE: For I have no one comparable to him for genuine interest in whatever concerns you.
text.DRB: For I have no man so of one mind that with sincere affection is careful for you.

text.NRSV: All of them are seeking their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, they all, their own things, do seek, not the things of Christ Jesus;
text.KJV: For all seek their own, not the things which are Jesus Christ’s.
text.NABRE: For they all seek their own interests, not those of Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: For all seek the things that are their own; not the things that are Jesus Christ's.

text.NRSV: But Timothy’s worth you know, how like a son with a father he has served with me in the work of the gospel.
text.Rotherham: But, of the proof of him, be taking note,—that, as child, with father, with me, hath he done service for the glad-message;
text.KJV: But ye know the proof of him, that, as a son with the father, he hath served with me in the gospel.
text.NABRE: But you know his worth, how as a child with a father he served along with me in the cause of the gospel.
text.DRB: And know ye an experiment of him, that as a son the Father, so hath he served with me in the Gospel.

text.NRSV: I hope therefore to send him as soon as I see how things go with me;
text.Rotherham: Him, indeed, therefore, am I hoping to send—as soon as I can look off from the things that concern myself,—forthwith.
text.KJV: Him therefore I hope to send presently, so soon as I shall see how it will go with me.
text.NABRE: He it is, then, whom I hope to send as soon as I see how things go with me,
text.DRB: This man therefore I hope to send unto you, immediately as I shall see the things that concern me.

text.NRSV: and I trust in the Lord that I will also come soon.
text.Rotherham: I am assured, however, in the Lord,—that I, myself, shall shortly come.
text.KJV: But I trust in the Lord that I also myself shall come shortly.
text.NABRE: but I am confident in the Lord that I myself will also come soon.
text.DRB: And I trust in our Lord that myself also shall come to you quickly.

text.NRSV: Still, I think it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus—my brother and co-worker and fellow soldier, your messenger and minister to my need;
text.Rotherham: Needful, nevertheless have I accounted it—Epaphroditus, my brother and fellow worker and fellow soldier, but your apostle and public minister to my need, to send unto you;
text.KJV: Yet I supposed it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus, my brother, and companion in labour, and fellowsoldier, but your messenger, and he that ministered to my wants.
text.NABRE: Epaphroditus. With regard to Epaphroditus, my brother and co-worker and fellow soldier, your messenger and minister in my need, I consider it necessary to send him to you.
text.DRB: But I have thought it necessary to send to you Epaphroditus my brother and coadjutor and fellow-soldier, but your Apostle, and minister of my necessity.

text.NRSV: for he has been longing for all of you, and has been distressed because you heard that he was ill.
text.Rotherham: Since he hath been longing to see, you all, and hath been in great distress, because ye had heard he was sick;—
text.KJV: For he longed after you all, and was full of heaviness, because that ye had heard that he had been sick.
text.NABRE: For he has been longing for all of you and was distressed because you heard that he was ill.
text.DRB: Because indeed he had a desire toward you all: and was pensive, for that you had heard that he was sick.

text.NRSV: He was indeed so ill that he nearly died. But God had mercy on him, and not only on him but on me also, so that I would not have one sorrow after another.
text.Rotherham: And, in fact, he was sick, nigh unto death; but, God, had mercy on him,—and, not on him only, but, on me also, lest, sorrow upon sorrow, I should have.
text.KJV: For indeed he was sick nigh unto death: but God had mercy on him; and not on him only, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.
text.NABRE: He was indeed ill, close to death; but God had mercy on him, not just on him but also on me, so that I might not have sorrow upon sorrow.
text.DRB: For indeed he was sick even to death: but God had mercy on him: and not only on him, but on me also, lest I should have sorrow upon sorrow.

text.NRSV: I am the more eager to send him, therefore, in order that you may rejoice at seeing him again, and that I may be less anxious.
text.Rotherham: The more promptly, therefore, have I sent him, that, seeing him again, ye may rejoice, and, I, the less sorrowful, may be.
text.KJV: I sent him therefore the more carefully, that, when ye see him again, ye may rejoice, and that I may be the less sorrowful.
text.NABRE: I send him therefore with the greater eagerness, so that, on seeing him, you may rejoice again, and I may have less anxiety.
text.DRB: Therefore I sent him the more speedily: that seeing him, you may rejoice again, and I may be without sorrow.

text.NRSV: Welcome him then in the Lord with all joy, and honor such people,
text.Rotherham: Be giving him welcome, therefore, in the Lord, with all joy; and, such as he, in honour, be holding,—
text.KJV: Receive him therefore in the Lord with all gladness; and hold such in reputation:
text.NABRE: Welcome him then in the Lord with all joy and hold such people in esteem,
text.DRB: Receive him therefore with all joy in our Lord: and such entreat with honour.

text.NRSV: because he came close to death for the work of Christ, risking his life to make up for those services that you could not give me.
text.Rotherham: Because, for the sake of the work of the Lord, unto death, he drew nigh, running hazard with his life, that he might fill up your lack of the public service, towards me.
text.KJV: Because for the work of Christ he was nigh unto death, not regarding his life, to supply your lack of service toward me.
text.NABRE: because for the sake of the work of Christ he came close to death, risking his life to make up for those services to me that you could not perform.
text.DRB: because for the work of Christ, he came to the point of death: yielding his life, that he might fulfill that which on your part wanted toward my service.

text.NRSV: Finally, my brothers and sisters, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you is not troublesome to me, and for you it is a safeguard.
text.Rotherham: For the rest, my brethren—rejoice in the Lord. To be writing, the same things, unto you, to me, is not irksome, while, for you, is safe:—
text.KJV: Finally, my brethren, rejoice in the Lord. To write the same things to you, to me indeed is not grievous, but for you it is safe.
text.NABRE: Chapter 3 Concluding Admonitions. Finally, my brothers, rejoice in the Lord. Writing the same things to you is no burden for me but is a safeguard for you.
text.DRB: From hence-forth, my brethren, rejoice in our Lord. To write the same things unto you, to me surely it is not tedious, and to you it is necessary.

text.NRSV: Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of those who mutilate the flesh!
text.Rotherham: Beware of the dogs, beware of mischievous workers, beware of the mutilation;
text.KJV: Beware of dogs, beware of evil workers, beware of the concision.
text.NABRE: V. Polemic: Righteousness and the Goal in Christ Against Legalistic Teachers. Beware of the dogs! Beware of the evil-workers! Beware of the mutilation!
text.DRB: See the dogs, see the evil workers, see the concision.

text.NRSV: For it is we who are the circumcision, who worship in the Spirit of God and boast in Christ Jesus and have no confidence in the flesh—
text.Rotherham: For, we, are the circumcision, who in the Spirit of God, are doing divine service, and are boasting in Christ Jesus, and, not in flesh, having confidence,—
text.KJV: For we are the circumcision, which worship God in the spirit, and rejoice in Christ Jesus, and have no confidence in the flesh.
text.NABRE: For we are the circumcision, we who worship through the Spirit of God, who boast in Christ Jesus and do not put our confidence in flesh,
text.DRB: For we are the circumcision, which in spirit serve God: and we glory in Christ Jesus, and not having confidence in the flesh,

text.NRSV: even though I, too, have reason for confidence in the flesh. If anyone else has reason to be confident in the flesh, I have more:
text.Rotherham: Although, indeed, I, might have confidence even in flesh. If any other thinketh to have confidence in flesh, I, more:—
text.KJV: Though I might also have confidence in the flesh. If any other man thinketh that he hath whereof he might trust in the flesh, I more:
text.NABRE: although I myself have grounds for confidence even in the flesh. Paul’s Autobiography. If anyone else thinks he can be confident in flesh, all the more can I.
text.DRB: albeit I also have confidence in the flesh. If any other man seem to have confidence in the flesh, I more,

text.NRSV: circumcised on the eighth day, a member of the people of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew born of Hebrews; as to the law, a Pharisee;
text.Rotherham: Circumcised, the eighth day, of the race of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrews,—regarding law, a Pharisee,
text.KJV: Circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, an Hebrew of the Hebrews; as touching the law, a Pharisee;
text.NABRE: Circumcised on the eighth day, of the race of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, a Hebrew of Hebrew parentage, in observance of the law a Pharisee,
text.DRB: circumcised the eighth day, of the stock of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, an Hebrew of Hebrews: according to the Law, a Pharisee:

text.NRSV: as to zeal, a persecutor of the church; as to righteousness under the law, blameless.
text.Rotherham: Regarding zeal, persecuting the assembly, regarding the righteousness that is in law, having become blameless.
text.KJV: Concerning zeal, persecuting the church; touching the righteousness which is in the law, blameless.
text.NABRE: in zeal I persecuted the church, in righteousness based on the law I was blameless.
text.DRB: according to emulation, persecuting the Church of God: according to the justice that is in the Law, conversing without blame.

text.NRSV: Yet whatever gains I had, these I have come to regard as loss because of Christ.
text.Rotherham: But, whatever things, unto me, were gain, the same, have I accounted, for the Christ’s sake, loss;
text.KJV: But what things were gain to me, those I counted loss for Christ.
text.NABRE: Righteousness from God. [But] whatever gains I had, these I have come to consider a loss because of Christ.
text.DRB: But the things that were gains to me, those have I esteemed for Christ, detriments.

text.NRSV: More than that, I regard everything as loss because of the surpassing value of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have suffered the loss of all things, and I regard them as rubbish, in order that I may gain Christ
text.Rotherham: Yea, doubtless! and I account all things to be, loss, because of the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord, for the sake of whom, the loss, of all things, have I suffered, and do account them refuse, in order that, Christ, I may win,
text.KJV: Yea doubtless, and I count all things but loss for the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus my Lord: for whom I have suffered the loss of all things, and do count them but dung, that I may win Christ,
text.NABRE: More than that, I even consider everything as a loss because of the supreme good of knowing Christ Jesus my Lord. For his sake I have accepted the loss of all things and I consider them so much rubbish, that I may gain Christ
text.DRB: Yea but I esteem all things to be detriment for the passing knowledge of Jesus Christ my Lord: for whom I have made all things as detriment, and do esteem them as dung, that I may gain Christ:

text.NRSV: and be found in him, not having a righteousness of my own that comes from the law, but one that comes through faith in Christ, the righteousness from God based on faith.
text.Rotherham: And be found in him—not having a righteousness of my own, that which is by law, but that which is through faith in Christ, the righteousness which is, of God, upon my faith,—
text.KJV: And be found in him, not having mine own righteousness, which is of the law, but that which is through the faith of Christ, the righteousness which is of God by faith:
text.NABRE: and be found in him, not having any righteousness of my own based on the law but that which comes through faith in Christ, the righteousness from God, depending on faith
text.DRB: and may be found in him not having my justice which is of the Law, but that which is of the faith of Christ, which is of God, justice in faith:

text.NRSV: I want to know Christ and the power of his resurrection and the sharing of his sufferings by becoming like him in his death,
text.Rotherham: To get to know him, and the power of his resurrection and fellowship of his sufferings, becoming conformed unto his death,—
text.KJV: That I may know him, and the power of his resurrection, and the fellowship of his sufferings, being made conformable unto his death;
text.NABRE: to know him and the power of his resurrection and [the] sharing of his sufferings by being conformed to his death,
text.DRB: to know him, and the virtue of his resurrection, and the society of his passions, configured to his death,

text.NRSV: if somehow I may attain the resurrection from the dead.
text.Rotherham: If by any means I may advance to the earlier resurrection, which is from among the dead:
text.KJV: If by any means I might attain unto the resurrection of the dead.
text.NABRE: if somehow I may attain the resurrection from the dead. Forward in Christ.
text.DRB: if by any means I may come to the resurrection which is from the dead.

text.NRSV: Not that I have already obtained this or have already reached the goal; but I press on to make it my own, because Christ Jesus has made me his own.
text.Rotherham: Not that I have, already, received, or have, already, reached perfection, but I am pressing on—if I may even lay hold of that for which I have also been laid hold of by Christ [Jesus]:—
text.KJV: Not as though I had already attained, either were already perfect: but I follow after, if that I may apprehend that for which also I am apprehended of Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: It is not that I have already taken hold of it or have already attained perfect maturity, but I continue my pursuit in hope that I may possess it, since I have indeed been taken possession of by Christ [Jesus].
text.DRB: Not that now I have received, or now am perfect: but I pursue, if I may comprehend, wherein I am also comprehended of Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Beloved, I do not consider that I have made it my own; but this one thing I do: forgetting what lies behind and straining forward to what lies ahead,
text.Rotherham: Brethren! I, as to myself, reckon that I have, not yet, laid hold; one thing, however,—the things behind, forgetting, and, unto the things before, eagerly reaching out,
text.KJV: Brethren, I count not myself to have apprehended: but this one thing I do, forgetting those things which are behind, and reaching forth unto those things which are before,
text.NABRE: Brothers, I for my part do not consider myself to have taken possession. Just one thing: forgetting what lies behind but straining forward to what lies ahead,
text.DRB: Brethren, I do not account that I have comprehended. Yet one thing: forgetting the things that are behind, but stretching forth myself to those that are before,

text.NRSV: I press on toward the goal for the prize of the heavenly call of God in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: With the goal in view, I press on for the prize of the upward calling of God in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: I press toward the mark for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: I continue my pursuit toward the goal, the prize of God’s upward calling, in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: I pursue to the mark, to the prize of the supernal vocation of God in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Let those of us then who are mature be of the same mind; and if you think differently about anything, this too God will reveal to you.
text.Rotherham: As many, therefore, as are full-grown, let, this, be our resolve; and, if, somewhat differently, ye are resolved, this, also, shall, God, unto you reveal.
text.KJV: Let us therefore, as many as be perfect, be thus minded: and if in any thing ye be otherwise minded, God shall reveal even this unto you.
text.NABRE: Let us, then, who are “perfectly mature” adopt this attitude. And if you have a different attitude, this too God will reveal to you.
text.DRB: Let us therefore as many as are perfect, be thus minded: and if you be any otherwise minded, this also God hath revealed to you.

text.NRSV: Only let us hold fast to what we have attained.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, whereunto we have advanced, in the same rank, stepping along.
text.KJV: Nevertheless, whereto we have already attained, let us walk by the same rule, let us mind the same thing.
text.NABRE: Only, with regard to what we have attained, continue on the same course. Wrong Conduct and Our Goal.
text.DRB: Nevertheless whereunto we are come, that we be of the same mind, let us continue in the same rule.

text.NRSV: Brothers and sisters, join in imitating me, and observe those who live according to the example you have in us.
text.Rotherham: Imitators together of me, become ye, brethren, and keep an eye on them who, thus, are walking,—even as ye have, us, for, an ensample.
text.KJV: Brethren, be followers together of me, and mark them which walk so as ye have us for an ensample.
text.NABRE: Join with others in being imitators of me, brothers, and observe those who thus conduct themselves according to the model you have in us.
text.DRB: Be followers of me, brethren, & observe them that walk so as you have our form.

text.NRSV: For many live as enemies of the cross of Christ; I have often told you of them, and now I tell you even with tears.
text.Rotherham: For, many, are walking—of whom I have often been telling you, and, now, even weeping, am telling,—the enemies of the cross of the Christ;
text.KJV: (For many walk, of whom I have told you often, and now tell you even weeping, that they are the enemies of the cross of Christ:
text.NABRE: For many, as I have often told you and now tell you even in tears, conduct themselves as enemies of the cross of Christ.
text.DRB: For many walk whom often I told you of (and now weeping also I tell you) the enemies of the cross of Christ:

text.NRSV: Their end is destruction; their god is the belly; and their glory is in their shame; their minds are set on earthly things.
text.Rotherham: Whose, end, is destruction, whose, God, is the belly, and [whose], glory, is in their shame, who, upon the earthly things, are resolved.
text.KJV: Whose end is destruction, whose God is their belly, and whose glory is in their shame, who mind earthly things.)
text.NABRE: Their end is destruction. Their God is their stomach; their glory is in their “shame.” Their minds are occupied with earthly things.
text.DRB: Whose end is destruction: whose God, is the belly: and their glory in their confusion, which mind worldly things.

text.NRSV: But our citizenship is in heaven, and it is from there that we are expecting a Savior, the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.Rotherham: For, our citizenship, in the heavens, hath its rise; wherefore, a Saviour also, do we ardently await,—The Lord Jesus Christ,—
text.KJV: For our conversation is in heaven; from whence also we look for the Saviour, the Lord Jesus Christ:
text.NABRE: But our citizenship is in heaven, and from it we also await a savior, the Lord Jesus Christ.
text.DRB: But our conversation is in Heaven: whence also we expect the Saviour, our Lord Jesus Christ,

text.NRSV: He will transform the body of our humiliation that it may be conformed to the body of his glory, by the power that also enables him to make all things subject to himself.
text.Rotherham: Who will transfigure our humbled body, into conformity with his glorified body, according to the energy wherewith he is able even to subdue, unto himself, all things.
text.KJV: Who shall change our vile body, that it may be fashioned like unto his glorious body, according to the working whereby he is able even to subdue all things unto himself.
text.NABRE: He will change our lowly body to conform with his glorified body by the power that enables him also to bring all things into subjection to himself.
text.DRB: who will reform the body of our humility, configured to the body of his glory, according to the operation whereby also he is able to subdue all things to himself.

text.NRSV: Therefore, my brothers and sisters, whom I love and long for, my joy and crown, stand firm in the Lord in this way, my beloved.
text.Rotherham: So then, my brethren, beloved and longed for, my joy and crown,—thus, stand fast in the Lord, beloved.
text.KJV: Therefore, my brethren dearly beloved and longed for, my joy and crown, so stand fast in the Lord, my dearly beloved.
text.NABRE: VI. Instructions for the Community Chapter 4 Live in Concord. Therefore, my brothers, whom I love and long for, my joy and crown, in this way stand firm in the Lord, beloved.
text.DRB: Therefore, my dearest brethren and most desired, my joy and my crown: so stand in our Lord, my dearest.

text.NRSV: I urge Euodia and I urge Syntyche to be of the same mind in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Euodia, I exhort, and, Syntche, I exhort,—on the same thing, to be resolved in the Lord:—
text.KJV: I beseech Euodias, and beseech Syntyche, that they be of the same mind in the Lord.
text.NABRE: I urge Euodia and I urge Syntyche to come to a mutual understanding in the Lord.
text.DRB: Euchodia I desire and Syntyche I beseech to be of one mind in our Lord.

text.NRSV: Yes, and I ask you also, my loyal companion, help these women, for they have struggled beside me in the work of the gospel, together with Clement and the rest of my co-workers, whose names are in the book of life.
text.Rotherham: Yea! I request thee also, true yokefellow, be thou helping together with these women,—who, indeed, in the joyful message, have maintained the combat with me, along with Clement also, and the rest of my fellow-workers, whose names, are in the book of life.
text.KJV: And I intreat thee also, true yokefellow, help those women which laboured with me in the gospel, with Clement also, and with other my fellowlabourers, whose names are in the book of life.
text.NABRE: Yes, and I ask you also, my true yokemate, to help them, for they have struggled at my side in promoting the gospel, along with Clement and my other co-workers, whose names are in the book of life.
text.DRB: Yea and I beseech thee my sincere companion, help those women that have laboured with me in the Gospel with Clement, and the rest my coadjutors, whose names are in the book of life.

text.NRSV: Rejoice in the Lord always; again I will say, Rejoice.
text.Rotherham: Rejoice in the Lord always: again, I will say—rejoice!
text.KJV: Rejoice in the Lord alway: and again I say, Rejoice.
text.NABRE: Joy and Peace. Rejoice in the Lord always. I shall say it again: rejoice!
text.DRB: Rejoice in our Lord always; again I say rejoice.

text.NRSV: Let your gentleness be known to everyone. The Lord is near.
text.Rotherham: Let, your considerateness, be known unto all men. The Lord, is near:—
text.KJV: Let your moderation be known unto all men. The Lord is at hand.
text.NABRE: Your kindness should be known to all. The Lord is near.
text.DRB: Let your modesty be known to all men. Our Lord is nigh.

text.NRSV: Do not worry about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God.
text.Rotherham: For nothing, be anxious, but, in everything, by your prayer and supplication with thanksgiving, let, your petitions, be made known unto God;
text.KJV: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
text.NABRE: Have no anxiety at all, but in everything, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, make your requests known to God.
text.DRB: Be nothing careful: but in every thing by prayer & supplication with thanks-giving let your petitions be known with God.

text.NRSV: And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, the peace of God, which riseth above every mind, shall guard your hearts and your thoughts, in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: Then the peace of God that surpasses all understanding will guard your hearts and minds in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: And the peace of God which passeth all understanding, keep your hearts and intelligences in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: Finally, beloved, whatever is true, whatever is honorable, whatever is just, whatever is pure, whatever is pleasing, whatever is commendable, if there is any excellence and if there is anything worthy of praise, think about these things.
text.Rotherham: For the rest, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things are dignified, whatsoever things are righteous, whatsoever things are chaste, whatsoever things are lovely, whatsoever things are of good report,—if there be any virtue, and if any praise, the same, be taking into account;
text.KJV: Finally, brethren, whatsoever things are true, whatsoever things are honest, whatsoever things are just, whatsoever things are pure, whatsoever things are lovely, whatsoever things are of good report; if there be any virtue, and if there be any praise, think on these things.
text.NABRE: Finally, brothers, whatever is true, whatever is honorable, whatever is just, whatever is pure, whatever is lovely, whatever is gracious, if there is any excellence and if there is anything worthy of praise, think about these things.
text.DRB: For the rest, brethren, what things soever be true, whatsoever honest, whatsoever just, whatsoever holy, whatsoever amiable, whatsoever of good fame, if there be any virtue, if any praise of discipline, these things think upon.

text.NRSV: Keep on doing the things that you have learned and received and heard and seen in me, and the God of peace will be with you.
text.Rotherham: The things which ye have both learned, and accepted, and heard, and seen in me, the same, practise;—and, the God of peace, shall be with you.
text.KJV: Those things, which ye have both learned, and received, and heard, and seen in me, do: and the God of peace shall be with you.
text.NABRE: Keep on doing what you have learned and received and heard and seen in me. Then the God of peace will be with you.
text.DRB: Which you have both learned, and received, and heard, & seen in me; these things do ye, and the God of peace shall be with you.

text.NRSV: I rejoice in the Lord greatly that now at last you have revived your concern for me; indeed, you were concerned for me, but had no opportunity to show it.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit I have been made to rejoice in the Lord greatly—that, now, at length, ye have flourished in your care for me,—although, indeed, ye were caring, but lacked opportunity.
text.KJV: But I rejoiced in the Lord greatly, that now at the last your care of me hath flourished again; wherein ye were also careful, but ye lacked opportunity.
text.NABRE: VII. Gratitude for the Philippians’ Generosity I rejoice greatly in the Lord that now at last you revived your concern for me. You were, of course, concerned about me but lacked an opportunity.
text.DRB: And I rejoiced in our Lord exceedingly, that once at the length you have reflourished to care for me, as you did also care: but you were occupied.

text.NRSV: Not that I am referring to being in need; for I have learned to be content with whatever I have.
text.Rotherham: Not that, as to coming short, I am speaking, for, I, have learned, in whatsoever circumstances I am, to be, independent:
text.KJV: Not that I speak in respect of want: for I have learned, in whatsoever state I am, therewith to be content.
text.NABRE: Not that I say this because of need, for I have learned, in whatever situation I find myself, to be self-sufficient.
text.DRB: Ispeak not as it were for penury. For I have learned, to be content with the things that I have.

text.NRSV: I know what it is to have little, and I know what it is to have plenty. In any and all circumstances I have learned the secret of being well-fed and of going hungry, of having plenty and of being in need.
text.Rotherham: I know [what it is] even to be kept low, and I know [what it is] to have more than enough,—in every way, and in all things, have I been let into the secret—both to be well fed, and to be hungering, both to have more than enough, and to be coming short:
text.KJV: I know both how to be abased, and I know how to abound: every where and in all things I am instructed both to be full and to be hungry, both to abound and to suffer need.
text.NABRE: I know indeed how to live in humble circumstances; I know also how to live with abundance. In every circumstance and in all things I have learned the secret of being well fed and of going hungry, of living in abundance and of being in need.
text.DRB: I know both to be brought low, I know also to abound: (every-where, and in all things I am instructed) both to be full, & to be hungry, both to abound, and to suffer penury.

text.NRSV: I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
text.Rotherham: I have might, for all things, in him that empowereth me.
text.KJV: I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me.
text.NABRE: I have the strength for everything through him who empowers me.
text.DRB: I can all things in him that strengtheneth me.

text.NRSV: In any case, it was kind of you to share my distress.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, nobly, have ye done, in taking fellowship with me in my tribulation.
text.KJV: Notwithstanding ye have well done, that ye did communicate with my affliction.
text.NABRE: Still, it was kind of you to share in my distress.
text.DRB: Nevertheless you have done well, communicating to my tribulation.

text.NRSV: You Philippians indeed know that in the early days of the gospel, when I left Macedonia, no church shared with me in the matter of giving and receiving, except you alone.
text.Rotherham: Ye know, moreover, even ye, Philippians,—that, in the beginning of the glad-message, when I went forth from Macedonia, not, so much as one assembly, with me had fellowship—in the matter of giving and receiving—save, ye alone,—
text.KJV: Now ye Philippians know also, that in the beginning of the gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no church communicated with me as concerning giving and receiving, but ye only.
text.NABRE: You Philippians indeed know that at the beginning of the gospel, when I left Macedonia, not a single church shared with me in an account of giving and receiving, except you alone.
text.DRB: And you also know, o Philippians, that in the beginning of the Gospel, when I departed from Macedonia, no Church communicated unto me in the account of gift and receit, but you only:

text.NRSV: For even when I was in Thessalonica, you sent me help for my needs more than once.
text.Rotherham: That, even in Thessalonica, both once and again, unto my need, ye sent:—
text.KJV: For even in Thessalonica ye sent once and again unto my necessity.
text.NABRE: For even when I was at Thessalonica you sent me something for my needs, not only once but more than once.
text.DRB: For unto Thessalonica also, once and twice you sent to my use.

text.NRSV: Not that I seek the gift, but I seek the profit that accumulates to your account.
text.Rotherham: Not that I seek after the gift, but I seek after the fruit that is to abound unto your account.
text.KJV: Not because I desire a gift: but I desire fruit that may abound to your account.
text.NABRE: It is not that I am eager for the gift; rather, I am eager for the profit that accrues to your account.
text.DRB: Not that I seek the gift, but I seek the fruit abounding in your account.

text.NRSV: I have been paid in full and have more than enough; I am fully satisfied, now that I have received from Epaphroditus the gifts you sent, a fragrant offering, a sacrifice acceptable and pleasing to God.
text.Rotherham: But I have all things in full, and have more than enough, I am filled, having welcomed from Epaphroditus the things that came from you,—a fragrance of sweet smell, an acceptable sacrifice, well pleasing unto God.
text.KJV: But I have all, and abound: I am full, having received of Epaphroditus the things which were sent from you, an odour of a sweet smell, a sacrifice acceptable, wellpleasing to God.
text.NABRE: I have received full payment and I abound. I am very well supplied because of what I received from you through Epaphroditus, “a fragrant aroma,” an acceptable sacrifice, pleasing to God.
text.DRB: But I have all things, and abound: I was filled after I received of Epaphroditus the things that you sent, an odour of sweetness, an acceptable host, pleasing God.

text.NRSV: And my God will fully satisfy every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
text.Rotherham: And, my God, will fill up your every need, according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
text.KJV: But my God shall supply all your need according to his riches in glory by Christ Jesus.
text.NABRE: My God will fully supply whatever you need, in accord with his glorious riches in Christ Jesus.
text.DRB: And my God supply all your lack according to his riches in glory, in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: To our God and Father be glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.Rotherham: Now, unto our God and Father, be the glory—unto the ages of ages. Amen!
text.KJV: Now unto God and our Father be glory for ever and ever. Amen.
text.NABRE: To our God and Father, glory forever and ever. Amen.
text.DRB: And to God & our Father be glory world without end. Amen.

text.NRSV: Greet every saint in Christ Jesus. The friends who are with me greet you.
text.Rotherham: Salute ye every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren who are with me, salute you.
text.KJV: Salute every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren which are with me greet you.
text.NABRE: VIII. Farewell Give my greetings to every holy one in Christ Jesus. The brothers who are with me send you their greetings;
text.DRB: Salute ye every saint in Christ Jesus.

text.NRSV: All the saints greet you, especially those of the emperor’s household.
text.Rotherham: All the saints salute you, but especially they who are of Caesar’s household.
text.KJV: All the saints salute you, chiefly they that are of Cesar’s household.
text.NABRE: all the holy ones send you their greetings, especially those of Caesar’s household.
text.DRB: The brethren that are with me, salute you. All the saints salute you: but especially they that are of Cæsar's house.

text.NRSV: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
text.Rotherham: The favour of our Lord Jesus Christ, be with your spirit.
text.KJV: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen. It was written to the Philippians from Rome by Epaphroditus.
text.NABRE: The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit.
text.DRB: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
